The Creosote bush (Larrea tridentata) is a small, shrubby plant native to the deserts of North America. The Creosote bush is known for its medicinal properties and offers several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Creosote bush include teas, capsules, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
